headline: Civil Rights Movement
description: The US Civil Rights Movement (1954-1968) fought to end racial segregation and discrimination. Martin Luther King Jr. led nonviolent resistance: Montgomery Bus Boycott (1955), March on Washington (1963, 'I Have a Dream'), Selma to Montgomery marches (1965). Landmark legislation: Civil Rights Act (1964), Voting Rights Act (1965).
